content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Zane Vorhes 
Attorney at Law 
2631 K street 
Sacramento, CA 
95816 
Dear Mr. Vorhes: 
December 20, 1985 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, 
Our File No. A-85-231 
Thank you for your letter requesting advice on behalf of 
San Juan Suburban water District Boardmember Glenn Miller. 
You 
have presented the following facts for our consideration. 
FACTS 
Mr. Glenn Miller is an employee of the Fair Oaks Irrigation 
District and has been duly elected to the San Juan Suburban 
water District Board of Directors, a public agency. 
San Juan 
Suburban water District is a California Community Services 
District. 
Fair Oaks Irrigation District is a public agency 
contained within the boundaries of the San Juan Suburban water 
District and purchases its water wholesale from the San Juan 
Suburban Water District. 
QUESTION 
Does the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 11 permit 
Mr. Miller to participate in Board actions taken on matters 
relating to the Fair Oaks Irrigation District? 
CONCLUSION 
Under the Political Reform Act, Mr. Miller may participate 
in Board actions taken on matters relating to the Fair Oaks 
Irrigation District. 
II Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
specified. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322,5660 


Zane Zorhes 
December 20, 1985 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
The Political Reform Act prohibits a public official from 
making, participating in, or using his or her official position 
to influence a governmental decision in which the official has 
a financial interest. 
Section 87100. 
Section 87103 provides 
in pertinent part that a public official has a financial 
interest in a governmental decision when it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material effect, 
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on: 
(c) 
Any source of income, other than gifts and 
other than loans by a commercial lending institution 
in the regular course of business on terms available 
to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more 
in value provided to, received by or promised to the 
public official within 12 months prior to the time 
when the decision is made. 
Mr. Miller is an employee of the Fair Oaks Irrigation 
District and receives a salary from that agency. 
However, the 
term "income" does not include salary and reimbursement for 
expenses or per diem received from a state, local, or federal 
government agency. 
Section 82030(b) (2). 
Therefore, Mr. Miller 
does not have a financial interest in the Fair Oaks Irrigation 
District for purposes of the Act. 
Accordingly, Mr. Miller is not required by the Act to 
disqualify himself from decisions which may affect the Fair 
Oaks Irrigation District. 
Please note, however, that our agency is not authorized to 
render advice on incompatibility of public offices. 
The 
Attorney General's office provides advice on possible conflicts 
arising from two public offices. 
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, 
please contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JGM:nwm 
Sincerely, 
(~ 
.;Y.l 0 71i~ ~a--
Jlohn G. McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


Jack B, Hansen 
General Manager and Secretary 
Zane Vorhes 
Attorney 
Dr, Frank B, Clendenen 
Consulting Engineer 
ORANGEVALE, CALIFORNiA 95662 
9935 AUBURN FOLSOM ROAD, ROSEVILLE, CALIF, 95678 
November 6, 1985 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Attn: 
Jeanette Turvill 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Dear Jeanette: 
(916) 969-2279 
(916) 791-0115 
DIRECTORS 
Robert R Sullivan 
President 
Kenneth H, Miller 
Vice President 
Mark E Yerke 
Albert C, Ricksecker 
Clois W, Snyder, Jr, 
A situation exists in the San Juan Suburban Water District 
resulting from the November 5th election wherein Mr. Glenn 
Miller, an employee of Fair Oaks Water District, defeated 
the incumbent and will now serve as a member of the Board 
of Directors of San Juan Suburban Water District. 
Fair Oaks Water District purchases wholesale water from San 
Juan Suburban Water District and is contained within the 
boundaries of said San Juan Suburban Water District. 
San 
Juan Suburban Water District was formed as the Master District 
for the purpose of serving the wholesale entities. 
We request an opinion from your office as to the perimeters 
of Mr. Miller's authority in regard to Board actions taken 
on matters relating to said Fair Oaks Water District. 
Your immediate attention will be appreciated inasmuch as Mr. 
Miller will assume office on November 29, 1985 and will be 
in a position to act on Board matters after that date. 
Thank you. 
Please call me if you have further questions. 
Sincerely, 
JBH: 11

TELEPHONE

Mr. Glenn Miller is an employee of the Fair Oaks Irri-
gation District and has been duly elected to the San Juan Suburban 
Water District Board of Directors. 
San Juan Suburban Water District 
is a California Community Services District. 
Fair Oaks Irrigation 
District is contained within the boundaries of the San Juan Subur-
ban Water District and purchases its water wholesale from the 
San 
Juan Suburban Water District. 
Mr. 
Glenn Miller 
and 
I 
would request a 
formal written opinion from your office as to 
the perimeters of Mr. Miller's authority in regard to Board actions 
taken on matters relating to the Fair Oaks Irrigation District. 
This authority has been spccificelly 
by Mr. Miller to this 
office pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b). 
If you have any questions, please feel free to give 
me a call. 
Sincerely, 
ZANE VORHES, INC. 
Is 
15 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jack B. Hansen, Secretary 
Board of Directors 
November 14, 1985 
San Juan Suburban Water District 
P.o. Box 85 
Orangevale, CA 
95662 
Dear Mr. Hansen: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-85-23l 
We are in receipt of your letter requesting advice 
regarding newly-elected board member Glenn Miller. 
As I 
explained over the telephone, we cannot provide you with advice 
at this time. 
Pursuant to Government Code section 83ll4(b) and 
our regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18329 (copy enclosed), 
we provide advice to the official whose duties or obligations 
are in question or to his or her authorized representative. 
If 
Mr. Miller wishes to seek our advice directly or authorizes you 
or Mr. Vorhes, the agency's attorney, to request advice on his 
behalf, we will be happy to respond if the request complies 
with the regulation. 
We look forward to the opportunity to serve you. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures

Zane Vorhes 
Sincerely,
